fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer occupational therapist license to new mexico, occupational therapist compact new mexico, new mexico occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address: New Mexico Board of Occupational Therapy, P.O. Box 25101, Santa Fe, NM 87504

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by July 1st of even-numbered years)
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in New Mexico.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to the RLD Online Licensing System, create an account, and complete the Occupational Therapist license application, providing all required personal and professional information.

steps.title: Submit Official Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your professional OT program and verification of your NBCOT certification to be sent directly to the New Mexico Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a state and federal Criminal Background Check (CBC) by following the instructions provided by the Board after submitting your application.

steps.title: Provide License Verifications (Endorsement Applicants)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement from another state, ensure verifications of any current or past OT licenses are sent directly from those respective state licensing boards to the New Mexico Board of Occupational Therapy.

steps.title: Pay Application and Licensure Fees
steps.description: Submit all applicable application and initial licensure fees through the RLD Online Licensing System to finalize your application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in New Mexico, create an account and complete the application through the RLD Online Licensing System.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the RLD Online Licensing System, click 'Sign up' to create an account with your email and password. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License' for Occupational Therapist.
